smart-contract.title: Blockchain and Solidity Foundations
smart-contract.description: This layer introduces how smart contracts run on the Ethereum Virtual Machine and the basic syntax of Solidity. Understanding these fundamentals is essential before writing any production logic.
smart-contract.challenge: Write and deploy a simple storage contract in Remix that stores and returns a number.

smart-contract.title: Contract Structure and Data Patterns
smart-contract.description: This layer covers how to organize contract state using structs, arrays, and mappings. Proper data modeling keeps contracts readable and gas efficient.
topics: Error handling with require and revert
smart-contract.challenge: Extend the storage contract into a simple registry that maps addresses to user profile structs.

smart-contract.title: Local Development with Foundry
smart-contract.description: This layer sets up a professional development workflow using Foundry and the Forge toolchain. A fast local environment is critical for iterating on contracts confidently.
smart-contract.challenge: Port the registry contract into a Foundry project and deploy it to a local Anvil node with a script.

smart-contract.title: Token Standards with OpenZeppelin
smart-contract.description: This layer teaches the standard token interfaces and how to build on audited OpenZeppelin contracts. Reusing battle-tested code reduces bugs and speeds delivery.
smart-contract.challenge: Create an ERC20 token that inherits from OpenZeppelin and add a capped supply.

smart-contract.title: Testing and Coverage with Forge
smart-contract.description: This layer focuses on writing thorough unit tests in Solidity using Forge. Strong test coverage is the foundation of safe contract development.
smart-contract.challenge: Write a full Forge test suite for your ERC20 token covering minting, transfers, and the supply cap.

smart-contract.title: Hardhat and JavaScript Tooling
smart-contract.description: This layer introduces the Hardhat framework and its JavaScript ecosystem for scripting and testing. Many teams use Hardhat for its plugin richness and frontend integration.
smart-contract.challenge: Recreate the token tests in a Hardhat project using ethers.js and run them against a local network.

smart-contract.title: Advanced Solidity Patterns
smart-contract.description: This layer explores advanced design patterns including upgradeable proxies and reusable libraries. These patterns enable maintainable and modular protocols.
smart-contract.challenge: Convert your token into an upgradeable proxy and ship a second implementation that adds a burn function.

smart-contract.title: Gas Optimization and EVM Internals
smart-contract.description: This layer teaches how to measure and reduce gas costs by understanding EVM storage and opcodes. Optimized contracts save users money and improve scalability.
smart-contract.challenge: Refactor your token contract to pack storage and use custom errors, then prove the gas savings with snapshots.

smart-contract.title: Building a DeFi Protocol
smart-contract.description: This layer applies prior skills to build a small decentralized finance protocol with oracles and incentives. Integrating external data and economic logic mirrors real production systems.
smart-contract.challenge: Build a staking vault where users deposit your token and earn rewards based on a Chainlink price feed.

smart-contract.title: Auditing and Mainnet Deployment
smart-contract.description: This final layer covers security review, verification, and shipping contracts to mainnet. Careful deployment and monitoring protect real user funds.
smart-contract.challenge: Run Slither on your staking vault, fix findings, and deploy a verified version to a public testnet.

ethereum-dev.title: Ethereum and the EVM
ethereum-dev.description: This layer explains how Ethereum works as a world computer and how the EVM processes transactions. A clear mental model of accounts and state is the basis for all later work.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Use a block explorer to trace a real transaction and document every field it contains.

ethereum-dev.title: Writing Smart Contracts in Solidity
ethereum-dev.description: This layer builds core Solidity skills for writing functional contracts. Knowing the language well lets you express on-chain logic safely.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Write a crowdfunding contract that accepts ether and tracks each contributor.

ethereum-dev.title: Connecting Apps with ethers.js
ethereum-dev.description: This layer connects browser and Node applications to Ethereum using ethers.js. Frontend integration turns contracts into usable products.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Build a web page that connects a wallet and lets users donate to your crowdfunding contract.

ethereum-dev.title: Running Nodes with Geth
ethereum-dev.description: This layer covers running an Ethereum execution client using Geth and querying it directly. Operating a node deepens understanding of the network and removes reliance on third parties.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Sync a Geth node on a testnet and query your crowdfunding contract through its JSON-RPC endpoint.

ethereum-dev.title: Token Standards and Contract Composition
ethereum-dev.description: This layer teaches the major token standards and how contracts call one another. Composability is what makes Ethereum a rich application platform.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Add an ERC20 reward token that your crowdfunding contract mints to contributors.

ethereum-dev.title: EVM Deep Dive
ethereum-dev.description: This layer dissects how Solidity compiles to bytecode and how the EVM executes opcodes. Low-level knowledge unlocks optimization and debugging skills.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Manually decode the function selector and calldata for a call to your reward token.

ethereum-dev.title: Indexing and Off-Chain Data
ethereum-dev.description: This layer shows how to index on-chain events for fast querying using The Graph. Efficient data access is essential for responsive dApps.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Build a subgraph that indexes contributions and display a live leaderboard in your dApp.

ethereum-dev.title: Wallets, Signatures, and Security
ethereum-dev.description: This layer covers cryptographic signatures and secure user interactions. Signature handling enables gasless approvals and meta transactions while avoiding phishing risks.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Implement EIP-712 typed signatures so users can approve reward claims without an on-chain approval transaction.

ethereum-dev.title: Testing and CI for dApps
ethereum-dev.description: This layer establishes automated testing and continuous integration for full stack dApps. Reliable pipelines catch regressions before they reach users.
ethereum-dev.challenge: Add a full test suite and a CI workflow that runs on every push for your crowdfunding dApp.

ethereum-dev.title: Mainnet Deployment and Operations
ethereum-dev.description: This final layer deploys the full dApp to mainnet and keeps it running safely. Operating in production requires monitoring, verification, and incident readiness.
topics: Gas and nonce management in production
ethereum-dev.challenge: Deploy and verify your complete crowdfunding dApp on a public network and wire up basic monitoring.

solana-dev.title: Solana Architecture and Accounts
solana-dev.description: This layer introduces Solana's high-throughput design and its account-based programming model. Grasping accounts and rent is fundamental to everything on Solana.
solana-dev.challenge: Use the Solana CLI to create a keypair, airdrop test SOL, and inspect an account on devnet.

solana-dev.title: Rust for Solana
solana-dev.description: This layer builds the Rust foundations needed to write Solana programs. Comfort with ownership and borrowing is required to write safe on-chain code.
solana-dev.challenge: Write a small Rust program that serializes and deserializes an account struct with Borsh.

solana-dev.title: Native Solana Programs
solana-dev.description: This layer writes a Solana program without frameworks to understand the raw runtime. Knowing the native model demystifies what Anchor does for you.
topics: Building and deploying with the CLI
solana-dev.challenge: Write a native counter program that increments a value stored in a program owned account.

solana-dev.title: Building with Anchor
solana-dev.description: This layer adopts the Anchor framework to remove boilerplate and add safety. Anchor is the standard way most teams build Solana programs.
solana-dev.challenge: Rebuild the counter as an Anchor program using account constraints and a PDA.

solana-dev.title: SPL Tokens
solana-dev.description: This layer covers the SPL token standard and how to mint and transfer tokens. Tokens are central to most Solana applications.
solana-dev.challenge: Add an Anchor instruction that mints an SPL reward token to a user each time the counter increments.

solana-dev.title: Client Integration with web3.js
solana-dev.description: This layer connects frontends to Solana programs using the JavaScript SDK and Anchor client. Client integration turns programs into real applications.
solana-dev.challenge: Build a web page that connects a wallet and calls your counter program with the Anchor client.

solana-dev.title: Testing Solana Programs
solana-dev.description: This layer establishes testing practices for Anchor programs locally. Thorough tests catch account and constraint bugs before deployment.
solana-dev.challenge: Write an Anchor test suite that covers counter increments and reward minting including failure cases.

solana-dev.title: Advanced Program Design
solana-dev.description: This layer covers advanced patterns such as composability, account resizing, and compute budgets. These techniques are needed for complex production programs.
solana-dev.challenge: Extend your program with a second program that it invokes via CPI to record reward history.

solana-dev.title: Security and Auditing on Solana
solana-dev.description: This layer focuses on common Solana vulnerabilities and how to defend against them. Account confusion attacks are a frequent source of exploits.
solana-dev.challenge: Audit your program for missing signer and owner checks and add constraints to close the gaps.

solana-dev.title: Mainnet Deployment and Operations
solana-dev.description: This final layer ships an Anchor program to mainnet and manages it over time. Production deployment requires careful upgrade authority and monitoring.
solana-dev.challenge: Deploy your full program and client to devnet and rehearse a safe program upgrade.

blockchain-core.title: Distributed Systems Foundations
blockchain-core.description: This layer establishes the distributed systems and cryptography concepts underlying every blockchain. These fundamentals explain why blockchains are structured the way they are.
blockchain-core.challenge: Implement a Merkle tree in Go and verify inclusion proofs for a set of transactions.

blockchain-core.title: Building a Minimal Blockchain
blockchain-core.description: This layer builds a simple blockchain data structure from scratch. Constructing blocks and chains by hand clarifies how state is linked and validated.
blockchain-core.challenge: Write a Go program that mines blocks with a simple proof of work and validates the chain.

blockchain-core.title: Rust for Systems Programming
blockchain-core.description: This layer introduces Rust as a second core language for safe, performant node software. Many modern chains are written in Rust for its memory safety guarantees.
blockchain-core.challenge: Reimplement your block validator in Rust using async tasks for parallel verification.

blockchain-core.title: Peer-to-Peer Networking
blockchain-core.description: This layer covers how nodes discover and communicate over a peer-to-peer network. Networking is the backbone that propagates blocks and transactions.
blockchain-core.challenge: Build a small libp2p network that gossips new blocks between several local nodes.

blockchain-core.title: Consensus Fundamentals
blockchain-core.description: This layer explains the consensus problem and classic algorithms for agreement. Consensus is what lets distrusting nodes agree on one history.
blockchain-core.challenge: Add a longest-chain fork choice rule to your network so nodes converge on one chain.

blockchain-core.title: Byzantine Fault Tolerant Consensus
blockchain-core.description: This layer studies BFT consensus protocols that deliver instant finality. BFT algorithms power many proof of stake chains today.
blockchain-core.challenge: Implement a simplified two-phase BFT round among your nodes that finalizes a block on a quorum.

blockchain-core.title: State and Storage Engines
blockchain-core.description: This layer covers how chains persist state efficiently using tries and key-value stores. Storage design directly affects sync speed and node hardware needs.
blockchain-core.challenge: Back your node's state with an embedded key-value store and add a state snapshot feature.

blockchain-core.title: Transaction Pool and Execution
blockchain-core.description: This layer builds the mempool and the execution pipeline that applies transactions to state. Correct ordering and execution are vital for deterministic results.
blockchain-core.challenge: Add a fee-prioritized mempool and a block producer that executes transactions deterministically.

blockchain-core.title: Security and Resilience
blockchain-core.description: This layer hardens the node against attacks on consensus and networking. Resilience is what keeps a live network safe under adversarial conditions.
blockchain-core.challenge: Add peer rate limiting and equivocation detection, then stress test your network against a misbehaving node.

blockchain-core.title: Testnet Launch and Operations
blockchain-core.description: This final layer launches a multi-node testnet and operates it like a real network. Running a live testnet validates the entire stack end to end.
blockchain-core.challenge: Launch a multi-validator testnet of your node, monitor it with metrics, and perform a coordinated upgrade.

blockchain-security.title: Smart Contract Security Mindset
blockchain-security.description: This layer introduces the adversarial mindset needed to review smart contracts. Thinking like an attacker is the core skill of every auditor.
blockchain-security.challenge: Review a small intentionally vulnerable contract and write up every issue you can find.

blockchain-security.title: Solidity Internals for Auditors
blockchain-security.description: This layer deepens Solidity and EVM knowledge from a security angle. Many bugs hide in low-level behavior that auditors must understand.
blockchain-security.challenge: Trace how a delegatecall changes storage context in a proxy and document the risk.

blockchain-security.title: Common Vulnerability Patterns
blockchain-security.description: This layer catalogs the most frequent exploit patterns with concrete examples. Recognizing these patterns quickly is what makes reviews efficient.
blockchain-security.challenge: Exploit a reentrancy bug in a sample vault on a local fork and then patch it.

blockchain-security.title: Static Analysis with Slither
blockchain-security.description: This layer automates vulnerability detection using the Slither static analyzer. Tooling lets auditors cover more ground and catch obvious issues fast.
blockchain-security.challenge: Run Slither on a mid-sized protocol and triage every finding into real or false positive.

blockchain-security.title: Fuzzing with Echidna
blockchain-security.description: This layer uses property-based fuzzing to find edge cases automatically. Fuzzers discover bugs that manual review and unit tests miss.
blockchain-security.challenge: Write invariants for the sample vault and run Echidna until it breaks an assumption.

blockchain-security.title: Manual Review Methodology
blockchain-security.description: This layer formalizes a repeatable manual review process. A systematic methodology produces consistent and defensible audit results.
blockchain-security.challenge: Perform a structured manual review of a DeFi contract and produce findings with proofs of concept.

blockchain-security.title: DeFi-Specific Attack Vectors
blockchain-security.description: This layer focuses on attacks unique to decentralized finance protocols. DeFi handles large value and presents complex economic risks.
blockchain-security.challenge: Build a flash loan proof of concept that manipulates a vulnerable price source and drains a pool.

blockchain-security.title: Formal Verification and Symbolic Execution
blockchain-security.description: This layer applies symbolic execution and formal methods to prove properties. These techniques give the strongest assurances about contract correctness.
blockchain-security.challenge: Use a symbolic tool to formally prove a key invariant of the sample vault holds for all inputs.

blockchain-security.title: Reporting and Disclosure
blockchain-security.description: This layer covers writing clear audit reports and handling responsible disclosure. Communication is as important as finding the bug itself.
blockchain-security.challenge: Compile your vault findings into a professional audit report with severities and remediation advice.

blockchain-security.title: Competitive Auditing and Continuous Monitoring
blockchain-security.description: This final layer prepares you for real engagements through competitive audits and on-chain monitoring. Ongoing vigilance protects protocols after launch.
blockchain-security.challenge: Enter a public competitive audit, submit at least one finding, and set up on-chain monitoring for a deployed contract.

chain-architect.title: Application-Specific Blockchain Concepts
chain-architect.description: This layer introduces the idea of purpose-built blockchains and the major frameworks for building them. Understanding the design space guides every architectural decision.
chain-architect.challenge: Write a design brief comparing Cosmos SDK and Substrate for a hypothetical application chain.

chain-architect.title: Consensus and Tendermint
chain-architect.description: This layer studies the Tendermint BFT engine that drives many app chains. Consensus choice determines finality, throughput, and security properties.
chain-architect.challenge: Run a local Tendermint network and observe how validators reach finality on each block.

chain-architect.title: Building Modules with Cosmos SDK
chain-architect.description: This layer builds custom state machine modules using the Cosmos SDK. Modules are where application logic lives in a Cosmos chain.
chain-architect.challenge: Scaffold a Cosmos chain and add a custom module that stores and retrieves a simple record.

chain-architect.title: Building Pallets with Substrate
chain-architect.description: This layer builds runtime logic using Substrate and the FRAME framework in Rust. Substrate offers a different, upgrade-friendly approach to chain design.
chain-architect.challenge: Build a Substrate pallet that mirrors your record module and trigger a forkless runtime upgrade.

chain-architect.title: Tokenomics and Governance Design
chain-architect.description: This layer designs the economic and governance systems of a chain. Incentives and on-chain governance shape long-term security and decentralization.
chain-architect.challenge: Design and configure staking and governance parameters for your Cosmos chain and submit a test proposal.

chain-architect.title: Cross-Chain Communication
chain-architect.description: This layer enables chains to talk to each other through interoperability protocols. Cross-chain messaging unlocks shared liquidity and composability across ecosystems.
chain-architect.challenge: Connect two local Cosmos chains over IBC and transfer a token between them with a relayer.

chain-architect.title: Modular Architecture and Data Availability
chain-architect.description: This layer explores separating execution, settlement, and data availability into distinct layers. Modular designs improve scalability and let teams specialize.
chain-architect.challenge: Diagram a modular architecture for your chain that uses a separate data availability layer.

chain-architect.title: Performance and Scalability
chain-architect.description: This layer tunes the chain for higher throughput and lower latency. Performance engineering is essential for chains expecting real usage.
chain-architect.challenge: Benchmark your chain under load and tune block parameters to improve throughput.

chain-architect.title: Security and Validator Operations
chain-architect.description: This layer secures the chain and defines safe validator operations. Operational security protects the network from downtime and slashing.
chain-architect.challenge: Set up a sentry node architecture with double-sign protection for a validator on your testnet.

chain-architect.title: Mainnet Launch and Upgrades
chain-architect.description: This final layer coordinates a mainnet launch and ongoing chain upgrades. Launching a sovereign chain demands careful planning and community coordination.
chain-architect.challenge: Run a genesis ceremony for a multi-validator testnet and execute a governance-approved upgrade.

bitcoin-dev.title: Bitcoin Fundamentals
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer explains how Bitcoin works as a peer-to-peer electronic cash system. The UTXO model and proof of work underpin everything built on Bitcoin.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Use a testnet wallet to send a transaction and trace its inputs and outputs on an explorer.

bitcoin-dev.title: Keys, Addresses, and Wallets
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer covers Bitcoin's cryptography and hierarchical deterministic wallets. Correct key handling is the foundation of custody and security.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Generate an HD wallet from a mnemonic and derive several addresses across derivation paths.

bitcoin-dev.title: Bitcoin Script
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer teaches Bitcoin's stack-based scripting language for locking and unlocking coins. Script defines the conditions under which bitcoin can be spent.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Construct and spend a multisignature script on testnet that requires two of three keys.

bitcoin-dev.title: Transactions and PSBT
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer builds raw transactions and works with partially signed Bitcoin transactions. PSBT is the standard for collaborative and hardware wallet signing.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Create a PSBT, sign it with two parties, and broadcast the finalized transaction on testnet.

bitcoin-dev.title: Running Bitcoin Core
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer runs and queries a full Bitcoin Core node. Operating a node gives trustless access to the chain and its RPC interface.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Run Bitcoin Core on signet and script a wallet workflow entirely through its RPC interface.

bitcoin-dev.title: Bitcoin Core Internals in C++
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer explores the Bitcoin Core codebase and its consensus rules in C++. Reading the reference implementation reveals how the protocol is truly enforced.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Build Bitcoin Core from source and add a small functional test that exercises a wallet RPC.

bitcoin-dev.title: Taproot and Advanced Scripting
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer covers Taproot, Schnorr signatures, and modern scripting capabilities. These upgrades improve privacy and flexibility for complex spending conditions.
topics: Key path and script path spends
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Create a Taproot output with both a key path and a script path spend and test both on signet.

bitcoin-dev.title: Lightning Network Fundamentals
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer introduces the Lightning Network for fast off-chain payments. Lightning scales Bitcoin payments to high volume and low fees.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Open a Lightning channel on testnet, route a payment, and then cooperatively close the channel.

bitcoin-dev.title: Building Lightning Applications
bitcoin-dev.description: This layer builds applications on top of a Lightning node using its API. Integrating Lightning enables instant payments inside real products.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Build a small app that generates invoices and accepts Lightning payments through a node API.

bitcoin-dev.title: Production Deployment and Security
bitcoin-dev.description: This final layer deploys Bitcoin and Lightning infrastructure securely for production. Custody and uptime demand rigorous operational security.
bitcoin-dev.challenge: Deploy a hardened Bitcoin and Lightning node setup with secure backups and uptime monitoring.

cosmos-dev.title: Cosmos Ecosystem Fundamentals
cosmos-dev.description: This layer introduces the Cosmos vision of an internet of blockchains and its core stack. Understanding the ecosystem clarifies why app chains and IBC matter.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Install the toolchain and spin up a single-node Cosmos chain locally to send a test transaction.

cosmos-dev.title: Go for Cosmos Development
cosmos-dev.description: This layer builds the Go skills required to work effectively in the Cosmos SDK. Comfort with Go and protobuf is essential for module development.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Write a Go package with protobuf-defined types and generate the corresponding Go code.

cosmos-dev.title: Cosmos SDK Architecture
cosmos-dev.description: This layer dissects how a Cosmos application is structured around modules and the baseapp. Knowing the architecture lets you place logic in the right component.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Scaffold a chain and trace a transaction from the ante handler through to state changes.

cosmos-dev.title: Building Custom Modules
cosmos-dev.description: This layer builds a custom SDK module with messages, state, and queries. Custom modules are where your application's unique logic lives.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Build a custom module that lets users create and query named items on your chain.

cosmos-dev.title: Tokens, Staking, and Governance
cosmos-dev.description: This layer integrates the standard bank, staking, and governance modules. These modules provide the economic backbone of any Cosmos chain.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Wire staking and governance into your chain and pass a parameter change proposal by vote.

cosmos-dev.title: Inter-Blockchain Communication
cosmos-dev.description: This layer connects your chain to others using the IBC protocol. IBC is the defining feature that lets Cosmos chains exchange value and data.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Establish an IBC channel between two local chains and transfer tokens across it with a relayer.

cosmos-dev.title: CosmWasm Smart Contracts
cosmos-dev.description: This layer adds WebAssembly smart contracts to a Cosmos chain using CosmWasm. CosmWasm enables flexible contract logic without forking the chain.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Write and deploy a CosmWasm contract that stores a counter and exposes execute and query messages.

cosmos-dev.title: Clients, Tooling, and Frontends
cosmos-dev.description: This layer connects frontends and services to a Cosmos chain using CosmJS. Client tooling turns your chain into a usable application.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Build a web frontend with Keplr that creates and lists items from your custom module.

cosmos-dev.title: Testing and Upgrades
cosmos-dev.description: This layer establishes testing and in-place upgrade workflows for a chain. Reliable upgrades let a live chain evolve without halting.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Write tests for your module and perform a governance-gated upgrade with a state migration on a testnet.

cosmos-dev.title: Mainnet Launch and Operations
cosmos-dev.description: This final layer launches a Cosmos chain to production and operates it reliably. A successful launch requires validator coordination and strong monitoring.
cosmos-dev.challenge: Launch a multi-validator testnet of your chain with IBC relaying and full monitoring in place.

move-developer.title: Move Language Foundations
move-developer.description: This layer introduces Move, a resource-oriented language built for safe asset handling. Move's design prevents whole classes of bugs common in other smart contract languages.
move-developer.challenge: Write and run a simple Move module that defines a basic struct and a function that returns it.

move-developer.title: Resources and Abilities
move-developer.description: This layer explores Move's core innovation of resources and the ability system. Abilities control how values can be copied, dropped, and stored, enforcing asset safety.
move-developer.challenge: Define a coin-like resource with the right abilities so it cannot be copied or silently dropped.

move-developer.title: Building on Aptos
move-developer.description: This layer builds and deploys Move modules on the Aptos blockchain. Aptos uses a global storage model that shapes how state is organized.
move-developer.challenge: Publish a Move module to Aptos testnet that stores a resource under a user account.

move-developer.title: Building on Sui
move-developer.description: This layer builds Move modules on Sui, which uses an object-centric model. Sui's objects and parallel execution differ significantly from account-based chains.
move-developer.challenge: Build a Sui module that mints an object and transfers ownership to another address on testnet.

move-developer.title: Tokens and Digital Assets
move-developer.description: This layer implements fungible and non-fungible assets using Move standards. Tokens are the most common building block of Move applications.
move-developer.challenge: Create a fungible token and a simple NFT collection on your chosen chain and mint examples.

move-developer.title: Testing and the Move Prover
move-developer.description: This layer covers unit testing and formal verification with the Move Prover. The prover can mathematically guarantee key properties of your modules.
move-developer.challenge: Write unit tests and a Move Prover specification proving your token cannot be minted beyond a cap.

move-developer.title: Composability and Generics
move-developer.description: This layer builds reusable, composable modules using generics and capabilities. Composability lets protocols build on each other safely.
move-developer.challenge: Refactor your token into a generic module and add a capability that gates the mint function.

move-developer.title: Frontend and SDK Integration
move-developer.description: This layer connects applications to Move modules using the TypeScript SDKs. Client integration turns modules into real user-facing products.
move-developer.challenge: Build a web page with wallet connection that mints and displays your token via the TypeScript SDK.

move-developer.title: Security and Auditing in Move
move-developer.description: This layer examines Move-specific security concerns and review techniques. Even with strong safety guarantees, logic and access bugs remain possible.
move-developer.challenge: Audit your token module for capability leakage and access flaws, then close any gaps you find.

move-developer.title: Mainnet Deployment and Upgrades
move-developer.description: This final layer deploys Move modules to mainnet and manages upgrades safely. Production deployment requires careful upgrade policy and monitoring.
move-developer.challenge: Deploy your full token application to testnet and rehearse a compatible module upgrade.

layer2-dev.title: Scaling and Layer 2 Foundations
layer2-dev.description: This layer explains why Ethereum needs scaling and how layer 2 solutions work. Understanding the scaling landscape frames every later design decision.
layer2-dev.challenge: Write a comparison of optimistic and zk rollups covering their trust and cost trade-offs.

layer2-dev.title: Deploying to Layer 2 Networks
layer2-dev.description: This layer deploys existing Solidity contracts to layer 2 networks. EVM-equivalent rollups let you reuse familiar tooling with lower fees.
layer2-dev.challenge: Deploy a sample ERC20 contract to an Optimism testnet and interact with it through an explorer.

layer2-dev.title: Optimistic Rollups in Depth
layer2-dev.description: This layer dives into how optimistic rollups achieve security through fraud proofs. The optimistic model trades a challenge window for low compute cost.
layer2-dev.challenge: Trace a withdrawal from an optimistic rollup to L1 and document each step of the challenge period.

layer2-dev.title: Zero-Knowledge Rollups
layer2-dev.description: This layer explores zk rollups and the validity proofs that secure them. Validity proofs offer fast finality and strong cryptographic guarantees.
topics: SNARKs and STARKs at a high level
layer2-dev.challenge: Deploy a contract to a zkSync testnet and compare its finality behavior to the optimistic deployment.

layer2-dev.title: Bridges and Cross-Layer Messaging
layer2-dev.description: This layer covers how assets and messages move between L1 and L2. Bridges are critical infrastructure and a frequent source of risk.
layer2-dev.challenge: Build a contract that sends a cross-domain message from L2 to an L1 contract on testnet.

layer2-dev.title: L2-Aware Contract Development
layer2-dev.description: This layer teaches how to write contracts that account for layer 2 specifics. Subtle differences in gas, fees, and opcodes affect correctness and cost.
layer2-dev.challenge: Optimize a contract for L2 by minimizing calldata and verify the fee reduction on a testnet.

layer2-dev.title: Building a dApp Across Layers
layer2-dev.description: This layer builds a full application that spans L1 and L2 with a frontend. Cross-layer apps must coordinate state and user experience across networks.
layer2-dev.challenge: Build a dApp that lets users deposit from L1, act on L2, and track their bridge transactions.

layer2-dev.title: Running and Customizing a Rollup
layer2-dev.description: This layer uses rollup frameworks to spin up a custom L2. Rollup-as-a-service stacks let teams launch their own chains quickly.
topics: Setting up a batcher and proposer
layer2-dev.challenge: Launch a local rollup using a stack framework and deploy your cross-layer dApp to it.

layer2-dev.title: Layer 2 Security and Decentralization
layer2-dev.description: This layer examines the security assumptions and decentralization roadmap of rollups. Sequencer centralization and upgrade keys are key risks to evaluate.
layer2-dev.challenge: Evaluate your custom rollup against the L2BEAT risk framework and document its weak points.

layer2-dev.title: Production Deployment and Operations
layer2-dev.description: This final layer ships a layer 2 application to a production network and operates it. Running cross-layer systems in production demands monitoring and safe upgrades.
layer2-dev.challenge: Deploy and verify your cross-layer dApp on a public L2 network and set up bridge and chain monitoring.
